title: verbascum × pseudolychnitis
intro: Verbascum × pseudolychnitis, also known as Mullein, is a biennial plant that belongs to the Scrophulariaceae family. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe respiratory issues, reduce pain, and boost the immune system. Mullein has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and insomnia, as well as to alleviate skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its flowers can be used to make a soothing oil or salve for topical application.
